clusters.title: Emotional scenes as more survivors pulled from flooded Laos cave
clusters.neutral_headline: Emotional scenes as more survivors pulled from flooded Laos cave
bullet_summary: A Laos rescue organization said​ that the water level inside the cave had receded enough for the four miners to leave with divers
Reported by Al Jazeera, CBS News, Guardian World and others

items.title: Emotional scenes as more survivors pulled from flooded Laos cave
items.standfirst: Divers in Laos have rescued four more villagers trapped in a flooded cave. The first survivor was pulled out on Friday.
items.content: Divers in Laos have rescued four more villagers trapped in a flooded cave. The first survivor was pulled out on Friday.

items.title: Four more Laos gold miners rescued from flooded cave
items.standfirst: A Laos rescue organization said​ that the water level inside the cave had receded enough for the four miners to leave with divers.
items.content: A Laos rescue organization said​ that the water level inside the cave had receded enough for the four miners to leave with divers.

items.title: 4 more men find way through Laos cave to freedom
items.standfirst: In Laos, four more gold miners were rescued from a flooded cave after being trapped there for over a week. Ramy Inocencio reports.
items.content: In Laos, four more gold miners were rescued from a flooded cave after being trapped there for over a week. Ramy Inocencio reports.

items.title: Four more men freed from flooded Laos cave in hazardous rescue mission
items.standfirst: Two still missing as divers make their way deeper into cave through muddy water and sharp rocks to find themFour more miners who were trapped in a flooded cave in Laos for 10 days have been freed by divers, but two people are still missing as rescuers continue to crawl through narrow, deluged tunnels and sharp rocks to find them.The first of the party of seven men was rescued on Friday in a perilous rescue mission which has required teams to drain water from the cave and navigate collapse hazards. Continue reading...
items.content: Two still missing as divers make their way deeper into cave through muddy water and sharp rocks to find themFour more miners who were trapped in a flooded cave in Laos for 10 days have been freed by divers, but two people are still missing as rescuers continue to crawl through narrow, deluged tunnels and sharp rocks to find them.The first of the party of seven men was rescued on Friday in a perilous rescue mission which has required teams to drain water from the cave and navigate collapse hazards. Continue reading...

items.title: Four more men freed from flooded Laos cave after 10 days
items.standfirst: Two of the seven villagers who entered the narrow tunnels in search for gold on 20 May are still missing.
items.content: Two of the seven villagers who entered the narrow tunnels in search for gold on 20 May are still missing.

items.title: Four more men rescued from flooded cave after becoming trapped while searching for gold
items.standfirst: Five men have now been freed after spending more than a week trapped in a flooded cave in Laos, according to rescuers, with two others still missing
items.content: Five men have now been freed after spending more than a week trapped in a flooded cave in Laos, according to rescuers, with two others still missing

items.title: Four people trapped in flooded cave in Laos pulled to safety
items.standfirst: Four people trapped for 10 days in a flooded cave in Laos have been safely evacuated by rescuers.
items.content: Four people trapped for 10 days in a flooded cave in Laos have been safely evacuated by rescuers.

items.title: Video shows first of five men trapped in Laos cave rescued
items.standfirst: The men from Laos have been trapped underground for over a week after entering a cave to look for gold. A team of rescuers is working to bring out all of the five so far located, although two others are still missing.
items.content: The men from Laos have been trapped underground for over a week after entering a cave to look for gold. A team of rescuers is working to bring out all of the five so far located, although two others are still missing.

items.title: Four still remain trapped in the flooded Laos cave after one person was rescued
items.standfirst: Rescuers have begun removing miners who have been trapped in a flooded cave in Laos for more than a week. One person was rescued while four others remain trapped. NBC News' Janis Mackey Frayer reports on the rescue operation underway.
items.content: Rescuers have begun removing miners who have been trapped in a flooded cave in Laos for more than a week. One person was rescued while four others remain trapped. NBC News' Janis Mackey Frayer reports on the rescue operation underway.

items.title: Man rescued after more than a week trapped in flooded cave in Laos
items.standfirst: Rescuers have begun removing miners trapped in a flooded cave in Laos for more than a week, with video showing the efforts as they ran late into the night local time.
items.content: Rescuers have begun removing miners trapped in a flooded cave in Laos for more than a week, with video showing the efforts as they ran late into the night local time.

clusters.title: Ebola spread in DR Congo 'deeply alarming', MSF warns
clusters.neutral_headline: Ebola spread in DR Congo 'deeply alarming', MSF warns
bullet_summary: Anaïs Legand, from the WHO’s high threat pathogens team, said the revised death rate estimate is based on confirmed cases
Reported by BBC World, Sky News World, NBC World and others

items.title: Ebola spread in DR Congo 'deeply alarming', MSF warns
items.standfirst: The medical charity's comments come as the head of the World Health Organization visits the region worst-hit by the virus outbreak.
items.content: The medical charity's comments come as the head of the World Health Organization visits the region worst-hit by the virus outbreak.

items.title: WHO chief heads to Ebola epicentre - as Brazil investigates suspected case
items.standfirst: The World Health Organisation (WHO) chief has visited the province hardest hit by the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC), urging residents to seek treatment and practice safe burials.
items.content: The World Health Organisation (WHO) chief has visited the province hardest hit by the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC), urging residents to seek treatment and practice safe burials.

items.title: Top W.H.O. official in Ebola epicenter as deadly outbreak spreads
items.standfirst: The head of the World Health Organization Saturday arrived in eastern DR Congo’s Bunia, a city at the heart of an outbreak of a rare type of Ebola, where the virus is still spreading faster than the response. NBC News’ Molly Hunter reports.
items.content: The head of the World Health Organization Saturday arrived in eastern DR Congo’s Bunia, a city at the heart of an outbreak of a rare type of Ebola, where the virus is still spreading faster than the response. NBC News’ Molly Hunter reports.

items.title: WHO chief visits eastern DR Congo amid Ebola outbreak
items.standfirst: The head of the World Health Organization (WHO) Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus traveled to Bunia, in DR Congo on Saturday to show his support to the residents of the capital of Ituri Province, the epicentre of a severe Ebola virus outbreak. There have been at least 1,077 suspected cases in the country since the outbreak was declared on May 15.
items.content: The head of the World Health Organization (WHO) Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus traveled to Bunia, in DR Congo on Saturday to show his support to the residents of the capital of Ituri Province, the epicentre of a severe Ebola virus outbreak. There have been at least 1,077 suspected cases in the country since the outbreak was declared on May 15.

items.title: WHO puts Ebola outbreak death rate at ‘huge’ 30-50% as chief arrives in DRC
items.standfirst: Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus calls for ceasefire among armed groups to help avoid deaths from preventable diseaseThe death rate of the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo is between 30% and 50%, the World Health Organization has said, as its head arrived in the country to support efforts to contain the disease.Anaïs Legand, from the WHO’s high threat pathogens team, said the revised death rate estimate is based on confirmed cases. “It’s huge. It means that up to five out of 10 people are likely to die,” Legand told reporters in Geneva. Continue reading...
items.content: Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus calls for ceasefire among armed groups to help avoid deaths from preventable diseaseThe death rate of the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo is between 30% and 50%, the World Health Organization has said, as its head arrived in the country to support efforts to contain the disease.Anaïs Legand, from the WHO’s high threat pathogens team, said the revised death rate estimate is based on confirmed cases. “It’s huge. It means that up to five out of 10 people are likely to die,” Legand told reporters in Geneva. Continue reading...

clusters.title: French police arrest more than 400 as PSG fans celebrate Champions League win over Arsenal
clusters.neutral_headline: Hundreds arrested in France after Champions League celebrations
bullet_summary: Seven officers were injured as football fans set off fires and vandalised shops, the interior minister, Laurent Nuñez, said, describing the violence as “absolutely unacceptable”
Reported by Guardian World, BBC World, Al Jazeera and others

items.title: French police arrest more than 400 as PSG fans celebrate Champions League win over Arsenal
items.standfirst: Interior minister says rioting took place in about 15 cities in France, with almost 300 people detained in ParisFrench police detained more than 400 people involved in violent clashes in Paris and other French cities that erupted on Saturday night after Paris Saint-Germain defeated Arsenal to win the Champions League title.Seven officers were injured as football fans set off fires and vandalised shops, the interior minister, Laurent Nuñez, said, describing the violence as “absolutely unacceptable”. One small group even tried to storm a Paris police station. Continue reading...
items.content: Interior minister says rioting took place in about 15 cities in France, with almost 300 people detained in ParisFrench police detained more than 400 people involved in violent clashes in Paris and other French cities that erupted on Saturday night after Paris Saint-Germain defeated Arsenal to win the Champions League title.Seven officers were injured as football fans set off fires and vandalised shops, the interior minister, Laurent Nuñez, said, describing the violence as “absolutely unacceptable”. One small group even tried to storm a Paris police station. Continue reading...

items.title: Hundreds arrested in France after wild Champions League celebrations
items.standfirst: Thousands of officers tried to quell unrest as crowds of fans - some firing flares - celebrated PSG's victory over Arsenal.
items.content: Thousands of officers tried to quell unrest as crowds of fans - some firing flares - celebrated PSG's victory over Arsenal.

items.title: France arrests hundreds of rioters nationwide as PSG win Champions League
items.standfirst: French Ministry of Interior says 416 people detained nationwide, including 283 in Paris, after PSG's win over Arsenal.
items.content: French Ministry of Interior says 416 people detained nationwide, including 283 in Paris, after PSG's win over Arsenal.

items.title: Hundreds detained in Paris as clashes erupt on PSG victory night
items.standfirst: More than 400 people on Saturday were detained across France, including 283 in Paris alone, after violent clashes erupted when thousands poured onto the streets after Paris Saint-Germain's Champions League final victory. It was not immediately clear how many of these individuals were remanded in custody to face further investigation.
items.content: More than 400 people on Saturday were detained across France, including 283 in Paris alone, after violent clashes erupted when thousands poured onto the streets after Paris Saint-Germain's Champions League final victory. It was not immediately clear how many of these individuals were remanded in custody to face further investigation.

items.title: The Papers: 'Agony for Arsenal' and '5 cops axed' at Kensington Palace
items.standfirst: Several of the papers focus on Arsenal's Champions League loss to Paris Saint-Germain on Saturday.
items.content: Several of the papers focus on Arsenal's Champions League loss to Paris Saint-Germain on Saturday.

items.title: Arsenal 'pain' will fuel fire after Champions League heartbreak
items.standfirst: On Sunday, Arsenal will get on an open bus parade to celebrate their Premier League title less than 24 hours after their Champions League final heartbreak.
items.content: On Sunday, Arsenal will get on an open bus parade to celebrate their Premier League title less than 24 hours after their Champions League final heartbreak.

items.title: 'He wanted to take it' - Gabriel's first Arsenal penalty ends with heartbreak
items.standfirst: Gabriel's first penalty for Arsenal ends in heartache as his shootout miss hands Paris St-Germain a second successive Champions League title.
items.content: Gabriel's first penalty for Arsenal ends in heartache as his shootout miss hands Paris St-Germain a second successive Champions League title.

items.title: Champions League final: PSG's players and fans 'kept on believing'
items.standfirst: PSG became back-to-back European champions by beating Arsenal 4-3 on penalties in a dramatic final in Budapest that ended 1-1 after extra time on Saturday. FRANCE 24's Selina Sykes reports from the Puskas Arena in Budapest.
items.content: PSG became back-to-back European champions by beating Arsenal 4-3 on penalties in a dramatic final in Budapest that ended 1-1 after extra time on Saturday. FRANCE 24's Selina Sykes reports from the Puskas Arena in Budapest.

items.title: PSG beat Arsenal to win back-to-back Champions League titles on penalties
items.standfirst: PSG win back-to-back Champions League titles with 4-3 shoot-out win over Arsenal following a 1-1 draw after extra time.
items.content: PSG win back-to-back Champions League titles with 4-3 shoot-out win over Arsenal following a 1-1 draw after extra time.

items.title: PSG wins back-to-back Champions League titles after shootout victory against Arsenal
items.standfirst: Paris Saint-Germain has won back-to-back Champions League titles by beating Arsenal on penalties in a dramatic final in Budapest.
items.content: Paris Saint-Germain has won back-to-back Champions League titles by beating Arsenal on penalties in a dramatic final in Budapest.(Image credit: Denes Erdos)

items.title: PSG retain Champions League with penalty shootout win over Arsenal
items.standfirst: Arsenal lose a penalty shootout as Paris St-Germain win the Champions League for a second successive year.
items.content: Arsenal lose a penalty shootout as Paris St-Germain win the Champions League for a second successive year.

items.title: Luis Enrique hails 'even bigger' win as PSG retain Champions League title
items.standfirst: Paris Saint-Germain's Spanish coach Luis Enrique became only the fifth manager to win three European Cups on Saturday as the Parisians prevailed in a tight final against Arsenal to win back-to-back Champions League titles.
items.content: Paris Saint-Germain's Spanish coach Luis Enrique became only the fifth manager to win three European Cups on Saturday as the Parisians prevailed in a tight final against Arsenal to win back-to-back Champions League titles.

items.title: PSG go back-to-back and join 'greatest of all time'
items.standfirst: Paris St-Germain underline their status as one of European football's greatest ever teams by retaining their Champions League crown with victory against Arsenal.
items.content: Paris St-Germain underline their status as one of European football's greatest ever teams by retaining their Champions League crown with victory against Arsenal.

items.title: PSG beat Arsenal on penalties to win back-to-back Champions League titles
items.standfirst: Defending champions Paris Saint-Germain beat Arsenal 4-3 on penalties to win their second consecutive Champions League title after a tense final in Budapest that featured more grit than flair, cementing their status among the giants of European football.
items.content: Defending champions Paris Saint-Germain beat Arsenal 4-3 on penalties to win their second consecutive Champions League title after a tense final in Budapest that featured more grit than flair, cementing their status among the giants of European football.

items.title: PSG and Arsenal chase European glory at UEFA Champions League final
items.standfirst: Arsenal and Paris Saint Germain will face off in a thrilling final under the lights of the Pushkas Arena in Budapest tonight. PSG has high hopes of winning the prestigious title for the second year in a row, whilst the Gunners are hoping to lift the trophy for the first time. Get a feel of the electric atmosphere inside the arena with FRANCE 24 journalist James Vasina.
items.content: Arsenal and Paris Saint Germain will face off in a thrilling final under the lights of the Pushkas Arena in Budapest tonight. PSG has high hopes of winning the prestigious title for the second year in a row, whilst the Gunners are hoping to lift the trophy for the first time. Get a feel of the electric atmosphere inside the arena with FRANCE 24 journalist James Vasina.

items.title: PSG hold on to Champions League title after dramatic shootout win over Arsenal
items.standfirst: Paris Saint-Germain won a second consecutive Champions League title on Saturday after edging Arsenal in a tense final that went all the way to a penalty shootout. Kai Havertz had fired Arsenal into a sixth-minute lead, but PSG's Ousmane Dembélé equalised from the spot before Eberechi Eze and Gabriel both missed for the Gunners in a dramatic finale. Read our blog to see how the match unfolded.
items.content: Paris Saint-Germain won a second consecutive Champions League title on Saturday after edging Arsenal in a tense final that went all the way to a penalty shootout. Kai Havertz had fired Arsenal into a sixth-minute lead, but PSG's Ousmane Dembélé equalised from the spot before Eberechi Eze and Gabriel both missed for the Gunners in a dramatic finale. Read our blog to see how the match unfolded.

items.title: Festive atmosphere in Budapest as PSG and Arsenal eye Champions League title
items.standfirst: It's the pinnacle of football. Kick off at the Champions League final in Budapest is just a few hours away. Arsenal are trying to win it for the first time, while Paris Saint-Germain wants to become only the second team to win it twice in a row. The atmosphere in Budapest is festive to say the least, details from the ground with FRANCE 24 journalist Selina Sykes and freelance sports journalist Karim Baldé.
items.content: It's the pinnacle of football. Kick off at the Champions League final in Budapest is just a few hours away. Arsenal are trying to win it for the first time, while Paris Saint-Germain wants to become only the second team to win it twice in a row. The atmosphere in Budapest is festive to say the least, details from the ground with FRANCE 24 journalist Selina Sykes and freelance sports journalist Karim Baldé.

clusters.title: Kanye West concert in Italy cancelled over ‘public order and safety issues’
clusters.neutral_headline: Kanye West concert in Italy cancelled over ‘public order and safety issues’
bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian World, BBC World, Sky News World

items.title: Kanye West concert in Italy cancelled over ‘public order and safety issues’
items.standfirst: Reggio Emilia prefect stops gig after Jewish community ‘concerns’ over rapper’s previous antisemitic remarksA Kanye West concert in Italy has been cancelled over “public order and safety issues”.The 48-year-old rapper, who changed his name to Ye in 2021, was due to perform at the Pulse of Gaia festival at the RCF Arena in Reggio Emilia on 18 July, but the city’s prefect, Salvatore Angieri, stopped the gigs after “concerns” from the local Jewish community over previous antisemitic remarks by West. Continue reading...
items.content: Reggio Emilia prefect stops gig after Jewish community ‘concerns’ over rapper’s previous antisemitic remarksA Kanye West concert in Italy has been cancelled over “public order and safety issues”.The 48-year-old rapper, who changed his name to Ye in 2021, was due to perform at the Pulse of Gaia festival at the RCF Arena in Reggio Emilia on 18 July, but the city’s prefect, Salvatore Angieri, stopped the gigs after “concerns” from the local Jewish community over previous antisemitic remarks by West. Continue reading...

items.title: Watch: Key moments from Nicola Sturgeon's interview with Laura Kuenssberg

items.title: Nicola Sturgeon: I feel as if I’m serving a sentence for a crime I did not commit
items.standfirst: Former Scottish first minister says she will not apologise for actions of her ex- husband found guilty of embezzlementNicola Sturgeon has said feels like she is “serving a sentence for a crime I did not commit” after her former husband and former Scottish National party chief executive, Peter Murrell, admitted embezzling more than £400,000 from the party.Murrell pleaded guilty this week to embezzling the sum from the SNP between 2010 and 2022 to fund a lavish personal lifestyle. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Middle East crisis live: Israeli army captures strategic castle in Lebanon in deepest incursion into country in 26 years
clusters.neutral_headline: Lebanon PM condemns Israeli 'scorched-earth policy' as fresh strikes hit south
bullet_summary: Israeli troops have captured and planted their flag on a strategic mountain topped with the Crusader-built Beaufort castle near the city of Nabatiyeh in southern Lebanon, the military said Sunday
Israel's defence minister said troops will remain at Beaufort as part of Israel's security zone in Lebanon

items.title: Middle East crisis live: Israeli army captures strategic castle in Lebanon in deepest incursion into country in 26 years
items.standfirst: Capture of Beaufort castle near the city of Nabatiyeh comes despite a nominal ceasefire and shortly before talks due in the USLebanon’s prime minister Nawaf Salam had accused Israel on Saturday of pursuing a “scorched-earth policy and collective punishment” in the south, urging a halt to the fighting and warning it was “destroying towns and villages, and forcing their inhabitants into exile”, AFP reports.Military delegations from both Israel and Lebanon held security talks in Washington on Friday, with more US-brokered negotiations planned next week. Hezbollah vehemently opposes the direct talks. Continue reading...
items.content: Capture of Beaufort castle near the city of Nabatiyeh comes despite a nominal ceasefire and shortly before talks due in the USLebanon’s prime minister Nawaf Salam had accused Israel on Saturday of pursuing a “scorched-earth policy and collective punishment” in the south, urging a halt to the fighting and warning it was “destroying towns and villages, and forcing their inhabitants into exile”, AFP reports.Military delegations from both Israel and Lebanon held security talks in Washington on Friday, with more US-brokered negotiations planned next week. Hezbollah vehemently opposes the direct talks. Continue reading...

items.title: Middle East live: Israel captures, plants its flag on medieval castle in southern Lebanon
items.standfirst: Israeli troops have captured and planted their flag on a strategic mountain topped with the Crusader-built Beaufort castle near the city of Nabatiyeh in southern Lebanon, the military said Sunday. It is Israel’s deepest incursion into Lebanon in more than a quarter century. Israel's defence minister said troops will remain at Beaufort as part of Israel's security zone in Lebanon. Follow our live blog for the latest updates.
items.content: Israeli troops have captured and planted their flag on a strategic mountain topped with the Crusader-built Beaufort castle near the city of Nabatiyeh in southern Lebanon, the military said Sunday. It is Israel’s deepest incursion into Lebanon in more than a quarter century. Israel's defence minister said troops will remain at Beaufort as part of Israel's security zone in Lebanon. Follow our live blog for the latest updates.

items.title: Israeli troops capture strategic Beaufort Castle as they push deeper into Lebanon
items.standfirst: Defence minister announces seizure of strategic ridge as advance against Hezbollah moves beyond Litani RiverIsraeli troops have captured the 900-year-old Beaufort Castle and its strategic ridge in southern Lebanon in a major advance against Hezbollah that took them beyond the Litani River – their deepest incursion into the country in more than 26 years.After days of intense fighting and airstrikes in nearby villages, the Israeli defence minister, Israel Katz, said the military had captured the fortress, also known as Qalaat al-Shakif, which the Israel Defense Forces used as a base during their previous occupation of southern Lebanon between 1982 and 2000. Continue reading...
items.content: Defence minister announces seizure of strategic ridge as advance against Hezbollah moves beyond Litani RiverIsraeli troops have captured the 900-year-old Beaufort Castle and its strategic ridge in southern Lebanon in a major advance against Hezbollah that took them beyond the Litani River – their deepest incursion into the country in more than 26 years.After days of intense fighting and airstrikes in nearby villages, the Israeli defence minister, Israel Katz, said the military had captured the fortress, also known as Qalaat al-Shakif, which the Israel Defense Forces used as a base during their previous occupation of southern Lebanon between 1982 and 2000. Continue reading...

items.title: Lebanon PM condemns Israeli 'scorched-earth policy' as fresh strikes hit south
items.standfirst: Lebanon's Prime Minister Nawaf Salam accused Israel in a televised address on Saturday of pursuing a “scorched-earth policy” following new Israeli strikes in the south of the country. A ceasefire between Israel and Tehran-backed Hezbollah officially took effect on April 17 but has not been observed.
items.content: Lebanon's Prime Minister Nawaf Salam accused Israel in a televised address on Saturday of pursuing a “scorched-earth policy” following new Israeli strikes in the south of the country. A ceasefire between Israel and Tehran-backed Hezbollah officially took effect on April 17 but has not been observed.

items.title: Wembanyama within sight of 'childhood dream' after Spurs stun Thunder
items.standfirst: French basketball prodigy Victor Wembanyama is within sight of realizing a lifelong dream after the San Antonio Spurs stunned the Oklahoma City Thunder Saturday, making their way to the upcoming NBA Finals showdown with the New York Knicks. The win marked another dazzling milestone on Wembanyama's rise as the new face of the NBA.
items.content: French basketball prodigy Victor Wembanyama is within sight of realizing a lifelong dream after the San Antonio Spurs stunned the Oklahoma City Thunder Saturday, making their way to the upcoming NBA Finals showdown with the New York Knicks. The win marked another dazzling milestone on Wembanyama's rise as the new face of the NBA.

items.title: Chicago-to-Minneapolis United Airlines flight diverted after attempted cockpit breach
items.standfirst: Plane landed in Wisconsin and ‘unruly passenger’ was taken into custody before flight continued to MinnesotaA United Airlines flight bound for Minneapolis from Chicago was reportedly diverted after an “unruly passenger” tried to breach the cockpit late on Friday.The FBI and police responded to reports of a security concern with the passenger, who was detained by police at the Dane county regional airport in Madison, Wisconsin. items.title: Iran reasserts control over Hormuz Strait as deal with US remains elusive
items.standfirst: Pentagon chief Pete Hegseth says US 'more than capable' of restarting war if satisfactory deal not reached.
items.content: Pentagon chief Pete Hegseth says US 'more than capable' of restarting war if satisfactory deal not reached.

items.title: US says 'more than capable' of resuming war, as peace deal remains elusive
items.standfirst: Pentagon head Pete Hegseth - has said that Washington could restart the war with Iran at any time if it wanted. His remarks follow days of talks and a two-hour meeting in the White House situation room - that has yet to produce a deal between the US and Iran. Analysis by FRANCE 24 international affairs editor, Shirli Sitbon.
items.content: Pentagon head Pete Hegseth - has said that Washington could restart the war with Iran at any time if it wanted. His remarks follow days of talks and a two-hour meeting in the White House situation room - that has yet to produce a deal between the US and Iran. Analysis by FRANCE 24 international affairs editor, Shirli Sitbon.

bullet_summary: Reported by NBC World, Guardian World

items.title: Truck carrying returning refugees crashes in eastern Afghanistan, killing 22
items.standfirst: KABUL, Afghanistan — A truck carrying Afghan refugees returning from neighboring Pakistan overturned on a highway in eastern Afghanistan on Saturday, killing at least 22 people and injuring about 36 others, most of them women and children, officials said
items.content: KABUL, Afghanistan — A truck carrying Afghan refugees returning from neighboring Pakistan overturned on a highway in eastern Afghanistan on Saturday, killing at least 22 people and injuring about 36 others, most of them women and children, officials said

items.title: Eighteen people killed in Afghanistan truck crash, including 10 children
items.standfirst: Truck was carrying Afghan families returning Pakistan when it overturned, official saysA truck overturned in eastern Afghanistan on Saturday, killing 18 people on board including 10 children, a provincial official told Agence France-Presse.Deadly traffic crashes are common in Afghanistan, due in part to poor roads after decades of conflict, dangerous driving and a lack of regulation. Continue reading...
items.content: Truck was carrying Afghan families returning Pakistan when it overturned, official saysA truck overturned in eastern Afghanistan on Saturday, killing 18 people on board including 10 children, a provincial official told Agence France-Presse.Deadly traffic crashes are common in Afghanistan, due in part to poor roads after decades of conflict, dangerous driving and a lack of regulation. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Iran official says Trump is stalling talks with ‘excessive demands’
clusters.neutral_headline: Trump claims to be on verge of peace deal but Iran signals no agreement reached
bullet_summary: Reported by NBC World, Guardian World

items.title: Iran official says Trump is stalling talks with ‘excessive demands’
items.standfirst: President Donald Trump has yet to make a decision on a deal that could extend the current ceasefire with Iran, with no announcement on Friday following a Situation Room meeting where he said he would make a “final determination.”Oil prices have dipped this week amid persistent reports that Trump and Iran could be close to sealing an agreement that has eluded the two sides for over a month
items.content: President Donald Trump has yet to make a decision on a deal that could extend the current ceasefire with Iran, with no announcement on Friday following a Situation Room meeting where he said he would make a “final determination.”Oil prices have dipped this week amid persistent reports that Trump and Iran could be close to sealing an agreement that has eluded the two sides for over a month

items.title: Trump claims to be on verge of peace deal but Iran signals no agreement reached
items.standfirst: US president did not announce decision on deal that could open strait of Hormuz after two-hour situation room meetDonald Trump has claimed he could approve an Iran peace deal on Friday that contains major concessions from Tehran, including the opening of the strait of Hormuz and the elimination of the country’s nuclear programme. However, top Iranian officials signalled a final agreement had not been reached.The two versions indicate Trump may once again be practising his “art of the deal” as he seeks to talk his way out of a war that has disrupted global energy supplies and rocked the world economy. Continue reading...
items.content: US president did not announce decision on deal that could open strait of Hormuz after two-hour situation room meetDonald Trump has claimed he could approve an Iran peace deal on Friday that contains major concessions from Tehran, including the opening of the strait of Hormuz and the elimination of the country’s nuclear programme. However, top Iranian officials signalled a final agreement had not been reached.The two versions indicate Trump may once again be practising his “art of the deal” as he seeks to talk his way out of a war that has disrupted global energy supplies and rocked the world economy. Continue reading...

items.title: Ex-US attorney general defends Epstein files handling in congressional probe

items.content: The testimony comes about a month after America's top prosecutor was ousted by US President Donald Trump.

items.title: Pam Bondi testifies behind closed doors in House committee's Epstein probe
items.standfirst: Former Attorney General Pam Bondi testified before the House Oversight Committee on Friday about her handling of the Epstein files.
items.content: Former Attorney General Pam Bondi testified before the House Oversight Committee on Friday about her handling of the Epstein files.

clusters.title: What’s Behind the Blue Origin Rocket Explosion?
clusters.neutral_headline: What’s Behind the Blue Origin Rocket Explosion
bullet_summary: Reported by Bloomberg Technology, Wired

items.title: What’s Behind the Blue Origin Rocket Explosion?
items.standfirst: Blue Origin’s New Glenn rocket exploded in a massive fireball while undergoing a test on a launchpad Thursday. Peter Elstrom explains what that means for the Bezos-backed company and its race with SpaceX. (Source: Bloomberg)
items.content: Blue Origin’s New Glenn rocket exploded in a massive fireball while undergoing a test on a launchpad Thursday. Peter Elstrom explains what that means for the Bezos-backed company and its race with SpaceX. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Blue Origin Rocket Explodes in Fiery Setback
items.standfirst: The detonation of the New Glenn rocket resulted in a huge fireball in Florida and may have long-term implications for the company's ambitions.
items.content: The detonation of the New Glenn rocket resulted in a huge fireball in Florida and may have long-term implications for the company's ambitions.
